description:
## Company Overview
**Founded in 1994, ETAS GmbH** is a wholly owned subsidiary of the Robert Bosch GmbH with international sales offices in Europe, North and South America, and Asia. ETAS' portfolio includes vehicle basic software, middleware, and development tools for the realization of software-defined vehicles. „Empowering Tomorrow’s Automotive Software“ – all our product solutions and services are focused on this to enable vehicle manufacturers and suppliers to develop and operate software-defined vehicles with increased efficiency. Holistic cybersecurity solutions in the automotive sector are offered via the ESCRYPT brand.

## Role Overview
ETAS GmbH is looking for an intern to support the development of automotive software solutions through user research and UI/UX design.

## What You Will Do
- Conduct **user research**, create **user flows**, **wireframes**, and **prototypes** for automotive software solutions.
- Translate user requirements and technical specifications into intuitive and visually appealing **UI designs**.
- Collaborate with developers to ensure the feasibility and proper implementation of UI/UX designs.
- Participate in **design critiques** and iterate on designs based on feedback and user testing.
- Present design concepts and rationales to internal stakeholders.
- Work with **AI-powered design tools** or integrate AI into design workflows.

## Requirements & Qualifications
- **Education**: Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree program in **Interaction Design, UI/UX Design, Human-Computer Interaction, Computer Science**, or a related field.
- **Portfolio**: Strong portfolio showcasing UX/UI design projects (academic or personal).
- **Tools**: Experience working with design tools such as **Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD**.
- **Knowledge**: Understanding of **user-centered design principles** and methodologies.
- **Soft Skills**: Ability to work independently and take initiative on assigned tasks.
- **Languages**: Fluent in **English** (written and spoken) is mandatory.

## Additional Information
- **Work Mode**: Hybrid
- **Start Date**: According to prior agreement
- **Duration**: 6 months
- **Application Requirements**: CV, transcript of records, enrollment certificate, examination regulations, and (if indicated) a valid work and residence permit.

## Corporate Culture
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in the corporate culture. All applications are welcome regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch (ETAS GmbH) is seeking a UX/UI Design Intern for a 6-month hybrid role focusing on automotive software. The intern will handle user research, wireframing, and prototyping using tools like Figma and Adobe XD, while integrating AI into design workflows.
title: Internship in UX/UI for Automotive Software Development

description:
## Company Overview
**At Bosch**, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: We grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Welcome to Bosch.

## Role Overview
Bosch is on a mission to harness the power of large-scale time-series data to optimize industrial processes and create innovative solutions. We are building the next generation of foundation models for time-series to be utilized across the various fields of the Bosch group, such as manufacturing, mobility solutions, energy systems, and IoT applications.

As a **Research Engineer**, you will focus on the efficient implementation of scalable architectures for large-scale time-series models. You will help push the state-of-the-art in model scalability by contributing to the core systems for our products.

## What You Will Do
- **Optimize training and inference pipelines**, enhancing their performance in terms of runtime and memory usage.
- Apply and refine scaled models for a diverse array of industrial use cases, leveraging massive datasets from Bosch's proprietary sources to solve real-world challenges.
- Design and optimize training and inference pipelines for time-series foundation models.
- Develop efficient algorithms for processing and analyzing massive industrial datasets with foresight and precision.
- Research and develop scalable architectures for large-scale time-series modeling in an industrial environment to set new standards.
- Drive continuous performance optimization and benchmarking of time-series models to ensure maximum efficiency.
- Implement and deploy scalable solutions for industrial time-series applications in close collaboration with engineering teams and business units.
- Publish and patent research results to secure and share technological advantages.

## Requirements

### Education
- Completed **Master's degree** in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Technical Cybernetics, or a related field.
- Supplemented by a **PhD** in Machine Learning or a machine-related field.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Demonstrated proficiency in **scalable machine learning architectures** and **distributed training** (including multi-GPU/multi-node training).
- Several years of experience in AI projects focused on **large-scale time-series data** from industrial domains (sensors, physical systems, IoT devices, simulations, etc.).
- Proven ability to translate theoretical methods into practical, high-quality code for scalable machine learning.
- Profound experience in **large-scale data processing** in an industrial environment.
- Excellent knowledge of **MLOps** (e.g., CI/CD pipelines, experiment tracking, containerization) in an industrial setting.
- Fluency in **statistical methods**, including time-series analysis and scalability optimization.
- Ability to translate theoretical methods into solutions for concrete engineering problems.

### Nice-to-Haves
- Proven ability in software implementations of large-scale algorithms in **Python/Pytorch**.
- Experience training and fine-tuning **foundation models** and **large-language models (LLMs)**.

### Personality and Working Practice
- Effectively communicate complex research results and collaborate seamlessly in cross-functional teams.

### Languages
- Very good written and spoken **English**.
- **German** is a plus.

## Benefits
- Flexible working models: various part-time options, mobile working, and job sharing.
- Inclusive corporate culture focusing on diversity and inclusion.

## Application Process
- Please submit all relevant documents (CV, certificates, and links to GitHub or Kaggle account).
summary: Bosch is seeking a Research Engineer with a PhD in Machine Learning to develop and scale foundation models for industrial time-series data. The role involves optimizing training/inference pipelines and implementing distributed ML architectures to solve real-world industrial challenges across mobility and energy sectors.
title: Research Engineer for Scaling of Large Time-Series Models (f/m/div.)

description:
## Company Overview
**Bei Bosch** gestalten wir Zukunft mit hochwertigen Technologien und Dienstleistungen, die Begeisterung wecken und das Leben der Menschen verbessern. Unser Versprechen an unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter steht dabei felsenfest: Wir wachsen gemeinsam, haben Freude an unserer Arbeit und inspirieren uns gegenseitig. Willkommen bei Bosch.

summary: Bosch is seeking a student for a 6-month mandatory internship in the Engineering Service Business, focusing on the application of control units for conventional and hybrid vehicles. The role involves conducting measurements on test tracks and test benches, analyzing data using INCA, and supporting the development of internal automation tools.

description:
## Role Overview

Do you want to transform your ideas into useful and meaningful technologies? Whether in the field of Mobility Solutions, Consumer Goods, Industrial Technology, or Energy and Building Technology – with us, you will improve the quality of life for people all over the world. Welcome to Bosch.

Bosch Service Solutions GmbH is looking forward to your application!

As a **Senior HW/SW Systems Engineer**, you are the driving force behind the development of central HW/SW components of our end-to-end **Stolen Vehicle Tracking solution** and their implementation in international customer projects. In addition to development, you take responsibility for certification and accreditation processes on an international level. Your expertise in telematics-based solutions and your ability to translate existing regulations into technical requirements are crucial for the success of this strategically important service.

## What You Will Do / Responsibilities

- Manage the development of our **Stolen Vehicle Tracking Hardware** from conception to series production.
- Responsible for the planning, monitoring, and execution of all necessary **accreditations and HW/SW certifications** (e.g., **Thatcham, SCM, UNECE, CE, RED, ECE R10, FCC**) in Europe and beyond.
- Coordinate testing laboratories, support tests through debugging, and ensure hardware and firmware compliance.
- Continuously monitor regulatory requirements and industry standards in the Stolen Vehicle Services and telematics environment to proactively ensure compliance.
- Advise the HW/SW development process and create/review relevant documentation (**test plans, reports, technical documents, declarations of conformity, installation guidelines**).
- Ensure product suitability for permanent vehicle installation and create corresponding installation guidelines.
- Perform **risk analyses** for hardware revisions and firmware updates and define the certification strategy for product changes.
- Support audits and technical customer tests.
- Act as a central interface between hardware/software engineers, product management, external testing labs/authorities, as well as sales, IT, and project management in international customer projects.

## Requirements

### Education

description:
## Role Overview
**Robert Bosch GmbH** is looking for a candidate for the **PreMaster Program - Development of Quality Agents**. You will be a key driver in promoting and implementing innovative Artificial Intelligence solutions within Bosch, providing significant value to the global engineering quality organization.

## What You Will Do
As a PreMaster in the **Vehicle Motion business unit**, you will be at the forefront of developing cutting-edge Quality Agents. Your primary focus will be on leveraging Workflow and Agentic solutions to support all product development projects for steering and braking systems.

## Responsibilities
- **End-to-end development** of Quality Agents: from conceptualization and design to implementation and continuous improvement in close collaboration with users.
- Develop an environment where agents can be managed efficiently with **increasing autonomy**.
- Moderate workshops and train partner departments.
- Collaborate with various stakeholders to ensure successful adoption.

## Requirements

### Education
- University degree (**Bachelor**) in computer science, engineering, or a related field.
- Committed to pursuing a **Master's degree**.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Strong interest and experience with **Artificial Intelligence** and **low-code/no-code platforms**.
- Strong command of **Microsoft tools**, particularly the **Power Platform** and **Copilot Studio** (highly appreciated).
- Programming skills are desirable.

### Personality and Working Practice
- Open-minded with strong communication skills and an eagerness to learn.
- Analytical approach and critical thinking abilities.
- Structured, self-directed, and results-oriented.
- Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team in an international context.

### Languages
- Fluent in both written and spoken **English** and **German**.

## Program Information: The Bosch PreMaster Program
The PreMaster Program is a two-stage qualification program for committed graduates with Bachelor's degrees who want to complete a Master's degree:
- **Phase 1**: Up to 12 months of practical experience as part of a gap year to learn technical and business contexts.
- **Phase 2**: The Master's course, including further practical experience via student employment and personal mentoring.

**Start**: According to prior agreement.

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in Bosch's corporate culture. All applications are welcome regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch is seeking a Bachelor's graduate for their PreMaster Program to develop AI-driven Quality Agents for the Vehicle Motion business unit. The role involves building agentic workflows using low-code tools and transitioning into a Master's degree with company support and mentoring.
title: PreMaster Program - Development of Quality Agents

description:
## Role Overview
At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. We are offering an exciting student internship position within our **Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS)** division. In this role, you will play a supporting part in the implementation and application of **Ratio Project Planning (RPP)** methodologies, contributing to the efficient and successful execution of our innovative ADAS projects.

This internship is a fantastic opportunity to apply your academic knowledge in a real-world engineering environment and learn about cutting-edge project planning techniques.

## Responsibilities
- **RPP Process Support:** Assist the RPP team in day-to-day tasks related to project planning and control, learning how RPP concepts are applied in practice within complex ADAS projects.
- **Data Collection & Preparation:** Support in gathering and preparing project-related data for RPP analyses, including cost, resource, and timeline information.
- **RPP Tool Familiarization:** Gain hands-on experience with RPP-related software tools and learn to utilize features for tracking and reporting, such as the "RPP Cockpit."
- **Methodology Learning:** Actively participate in discussions and workshops to understand RPP principles, including "Planning in Ratios" and "RPP Gates."
- **Documentation Assistance:** Help in maintaining and updating RPP documentation, templates, and best practice guidelines.
- **Ad-hoc Analysis:** Conduct basic data analysis and generate reports to support project managers and RPP specialists in decision-making.
- **Cross-functional Exposure:** Collaborate with various technical and project teams, gaining insights into the interdependencies of ADAS development.
- **Contribution to Efficiency:** Identify opportunities for process improvements and contribute ideas to enhance project efficiency.

## Qualifications

### Educational Background
- Currently enrolled as a student in a Bachelor's program in **Electrical Engineering, Information Technology, Mechatronics, Industrial Engineering**, or a closely related field.
- Expected graduation no earlier than **2027**, mid-program student seeking practical experience.

### Technical Foundations
- Strong understanding of electrotechnical and information technology fundamentals.
- Familiarity with digital systems and logic.
- Demonstrated system-level understanding of technical systems.

### Practical Experience
- Initial experience in project-oriented work, ideally through student assistant roles.
- Experience in data processing or administrative support is a plus.

### Software & Programming Skills
- Basic programming skills, particularly in **C++** and **Python**.
- Proficient in **MS Excel** for data handling and basic analysis.
- Familiarity with simulation tools like **LTspice** is beneficial.

### Language Skills
- **German:** Fluent (mother tongue).
- **English:** Excellent skills (C1 level or equivalent).
- **French:** Basic skills (B1 level) are a welcome addition.

### Personal Attributes
- Highly analytical with a keen eye for detail and a structured approach to problem-solving.
- Proactive, curious, and eager to learn new methodologies and tools.
- Strong communication and teamwork skills.
- Ability to quickly adapt to new tasks and absorb complex information.
- Genuine interest in project management, process optimization, and the automotive industry.

## Additional Information
- **Start:** month year / according to prior agreement
- **Duration:** 3 - 6 months
- **Requirement:** Enrollment at university. 
- **Application Documents:** CV, transcript of records, enrollment certificate, examination regulations, and valid work and residence permit (if indicated).

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in our corporate culture. We welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch is seeking a technically curious student for an internship within their ADAS division to support Ratio Project Planning (RPP) methodologies. The role involves data collection, tool utilization, and process optimization to support the execution of automotive projects.
title: Internship in ADAS Product Lifecycle Management and RPP

description:
## Company Overview
**Bei Bosch** gestalten wir Zukunft mit hochwertigen Technologien und Dienstleistungen, die Begeisterung wecken und das Leben der Menschen verbessern. Unser Versprechen an unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter steht dabei felsenfest: Wir wachsen gemeinsam, haben Freude an unserer Arbeit und inspirieren uns gegenseitig. Willkommen bei Bosch.

## Role Overview
As a **PreMaster Student**, you will support an experienced team in the development of equipment and process strategies within the **APC - Advanced Process Control** project in Semiconductor Manufacturing.

## What You Will Do
- **Strategy Development**: Support the development of equipment and process strategies.
- **Data Analysis**: Analyze equipment and process parameters, create control strategies based on data, and develop models for predicting and classifying process deviations.
- **Optimization**: Work closely with Engineering in the analysis and optimization area, independently analyzing large amounts of data using modern methods to identify potentials for increasing quality and yield.
- **Implementation**: Accompany the successful implementation and validation of developed strategies in a real production environment and ensure their sustainable operation.

## Requirements

### Education
- Completed university degree (**Bachelor**) in **Engineering, Computer Science**, or a comparable course of study.
- Concrete intention to pursue a **Master's degree** following the program.

### Personality and Working Style
- Self-initiated, independent, team player, capable of handling conflict, creative, structured, and flexible.

### Experience and Know-how
- Knowledge in the field of **semiconductor production** or **programming skills** (advantageous).

### Languages
- Very good **German** and **English** (written and spoken).

## The PreMaster Program
The Bosch PreMaster Program is a two-stage qualification program for committed Bachelor graduates aiming to complete a Master's degree:
- **Phase 1**: Up to 12 months of practical experience as a Gap Year to learn technical and entrepreneurial contexts.
- **Phase 2**: The Master's study phase, including further practical insights through student employment and personal mentoring toward a successful graduation.

## Additional Information
- **Start Date**: By arrangement.
- **Diversity & Inclusion**: Bosch welcomes all applications regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Contact Persons
- **Application Process**: Johanna Ripple (+49 7121 35-34427)
- **Technical Questions**: Ioannis Sotiriadis (+49 7121 35-12106)
summary: Bosch is seeking a Bachelor graduate in Engineering or Computer Science for their PreMaster program in Semiconductor Manufacturing (APC project). The role involves data analysis, developing process control strategies, and implementing models in a production environment as part of a two-stage qualification path toward a Master's degree.

description:
## Role Overview

**Möchten Sie Ihre Ideen in nutzbringende und sinnvolle Technologien verwandeln?** Ob im Bereich Mobility Solutions, Consumer Goods, Industrial Technology oder Energy and Building Technology – mit uns verbessern Sie die Lebensqualität der Menschen auf der ganzen Welt. Willkommen bei Bosch.

Die Bosch Service Solutions GmbH freut sich auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Werde Teil unseres Teams als **IT Product Security Specialist** und leiste einen bedeutenden Beitrag, indem du die Sicherheit unserer vielfältigen IT‑Landschaft – von lokalen Rechenzentren bis hin zu Multi‑Cloud‑Plattformen – konzipierst und verantwortest.

## Responsibilities

- **Lead the Product Security Program**: Integrate security into the SDLC, perform Threat Modeling, and derive security requirements for features and architecture changes.
- **Risk Analysis**: Conduct risk analyses and prioritize product-related risks (Design, dependencies, compliance).
- **Product Roadmap Integration**: Work closely with Product Managers to integrate security into product roadmaps and planning.
- **Governance Liaison**: Act as an intermediary between governance and technical implementation; define security guidelines and actively participate in their implementation and enforcement across various products.
- **Tooling & Automation**: Develop and implement secure tooling and automation processes in CI/CD pipelines (SCA, SAST/DAST, Secret Scanning).
- **Vulnerability Management**: Manage Vulnerability Triage and Remediation SLAs and participate in Incident Response for product security incidents (Root Cause Analysis, customer communication, long-term measures).
- **Compliance Monitoring**: Monitor new regulatory requirements (e.g., EU Cyber Resilience Act, NIS2, Cybersecurity Labeling) and ensure compliance.
- **Enablement**: Create and conduct security trainings, guidelines, and enablement initiatives for engineering and product teams.
- **Reporting**: Report relevant security metrics and program status to management.

## Requirements

### Professional Experience
- **5–10 years** of experience in Product Security, Application Security, or roles with a focus on Secure SDLC.

### Technical Know-how
- Solid technical background in **Software Development, Security Engineering, or Architecture**.
- Comprehensive knowledge of threats and countermeasures (**OWASP**, Secure Design Patterns, authentication models, supply chain risks).
- Experience with **Cloud Security** or relevant standards/regulations (e.g., **NIST SSDF, ISO 21434**).
- Practical experience in **Threat Modeling (STRIDE** or similar) and the use of risk assessment frameworks.
- Proficiency with development tools as well as **agile and DevOps** environments.

### Skills
- Strong stakeholder communication across all levels to ensure clear alignment and effective collaboration.

### Personal Characteristics
- High self-motivation and independence.
- Strong sense of responsibility.
- Proactive way of working.

### Certifications
- **CISM** or **CISSP** are an advantage, but not mandatory.

### Languages
- Fluent **German** and **English** skills (written and spoken).

## Contact Persons
- **Department**: Mr. Andreas Thiele
- **HR**: Ms. Simone Hotz
summary: Bosch is seeking an IT Product Security Specialist to lead their Product Security Program, focusing on integrating security into the SDLC, threat modeling, and ensuring compliance with regulations like NIS2. The role involves managing CI/CD security tooling and coordinating between governance and technical execution across multi-cloud and on-prem environments.
title: IT Product Security Specialist (m/w/div.)

description:
## Role Overview
Do you want to transform your ideas into beneficial and meaningful technologies? Whether in the field of Mobility Solutions, Consumer Goods, Industrial Technology, or Energy and Building Technology – with us, you improve the quality of life for people all over the world. Welcome to Bosch.

In this key role, you are the central interface between our business unit and the Bosch corporate headquarters. You ensure that our products comply with all relevant national and international compliance requirements from conception to market launch and beyond.

## Responsibilities
- Responsible for **Product Compliance**, **Product Engineering**, and **Product Cybersecurity** within Bosch SO.
- Provide strategic consulting as an expert for development teams, product management, and business unit management on all product compliance issues (e.g., **Cybersecurity, Safety, AI, CE marking, REACH, RoHS, WEEE, Product Safety Act**, specific industry standards).
- Identify, analyze, and interpret relevant national and international laws, regulations, and standards for products and translate them into concrete requirements for product development.
- Act as the primary contact for product compliance topics between the business unit and the Bosch corporate headquarters, coordinating strategies and ensuring consistent implementation of group-wide guidelines.
- Monitor the implementation of essential minimum requirements for the **Product Compliance Management System (PCMS)** according to RBGF182.
- Develop and implement product engineering and compliance processes and tools within the business unit; responsible for the further development of the **SOCD09010 (Product Engineering Process)** in coordination with other central functions (e.g., QMM, PM, UX).
- Identify potential compliance risks in product development, production, and sales and develop risk mitigation strategies.
- Train business unit employees on relevant compliance topics and promote high awareness of product safety and conformity.
- Annually review the product compliance implementation of 2-3 products.
- Support **M&A and PMI processes** in the area of product compliance.

## Requirements

### Education
- Successfully completed degree in **Engineering, Law, Industrial Engineering**, or a comparable qualification.

### Professional Experience
- Several years of relevant professional experience in product development, product certification, or quality assurance, ideally in an international environment.

### Know-how
- Sound knowledge of relevant national and international laws and regulations (e.g., **Product Safety Act, CE directives, REACH, RoHS, WEEE**, etc.).
- Specific knowledge of relevant standards/laws for the business area (e.g., **Automotive standards, IEC 61508, MedGV**) is an advantage.

### Skills
- Strong communication and moderation skills to convey complex issues clearly and target-group specifically.
- Ability to interact successfully with various stakeholders internally and with corporate headquarters.
- High analytical skills and the ability to quickly grasp complex issues and act solution-oriented.

### Personal Characteristics
- Initiative and sense of responsibility.
- Independent, structured, and result-oriented way of working.

### Languages
- **Business fluent German and English** (written and spoken) are mandatory.
- Additional language skills are an advantage.

### Other
- Occasional willingness to travel (national and international) is required.

## Contact
HR: Ms. Simone Hotz
summary: Bosch is seeking a Product Engineering and Compliance Officer to act as the primary link between their business unit and corporate headquarters. The role involves managing product compliance, cybersecurity, and engineering processes (including PCMS and SOCD09010) to ensure all products meet national and international legal standards.
title: Product Engineering and Compliance Officer (m/w/div)

description:
## Company Overview
**Bosch Industriekessel GmbH**, a company of Bosch Thermotechnik GmbH, is one of the world's leading manufacturers of industrial large-scale boiler systems based in Gunzenhausen, northern Bavaria. As a competence center for Bosch Thermotechnik, they develop and manufacture large boilers and other system components, distributing products in over 140 countries.

## Role Overview
As a **Working Student in Mechanical Development**, you will help shape the sustainable energy technology of tomorrow. You will dive deep into the world of electrode boilers and contribute significantly to the selection and integration of central system components.

## What You Will Do
- Take joint responsibility for the **selection and integration of central system components** to ensure the efficiency and reliability of electrode boilers.
- Research and evaluate components such as **make-up water systems (Nachspeisung) and pressure maintenance (Druckhaltung)** to find optimal solutions.
- Identify suitable **suppliers**.
- Actively contribute your knowledge to the **technical design** of systems and support the engineering team.
- Precisely define the **mechanical and electrical interfaces** for Bosch systems.

## Requirements
- **Education:** Degree studies in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Environmental Technology, Process Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a comparable field.
- **Experience and Know-how:** Initial experience in **plant engineering (Anlagenbau)** is an advantage.
- **Personality and Working Style:**
    - High motivation and ability to communicate ideas clearly and understandably.
    - Ability to integrate easily into a team and take responsibility for tasks.
    - Independent and structured way of working.
- **Languages:** Business fluent **German** and very good **English**.
- **Administrative:** Enrollment at a university is a prerequisite for the working student position.

## Working Conditions
- **Start Date:** By arrangement
- **Duration:** 12 months
- **Hours:** 15 hours per week
- **Work Mode:** Presence on-site is required; partial mobile working is possible.

## Diversity and Inclusion
Diversity and inclusion are deeply anchored in the company culture. Applications are welcomed regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Contact for Technical Questions
Jakob Geißendörfer (Technical Department)
Phone: +49 9831 56 393
summary: Bosch is seeking a working student for mechanical development to assist in the selection, integration, and technical design of components for industrial electrode boilers. The role involves researching system components, identifying suppliers, and defining mechanical/electrical interfaces in Gunzenhausen.
title: Werkstudent Mechanische Entwicklung (w/m/div.)

We invent, code and create digital products, services as well as solutions that spark enthusiasm and improve quality of life. description:
## Company Overview
At **Bosch**, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference.

## Role Overview
Robert Bosch GmbH is seeking an intern to gain hands-on experience in the development and automation of testing equipment for a range of products, including **MEMS sensors, IoT devices, and metering equipment**.

## What You Will Do
- Gain experience in the development and automation of testing equipment.
- Familiarize yourself with the testing of MEMS sensors by applying and controlling stimuli such as **acceleration, pressure, temperature, vibrations, and acoustics**.

- Participate in the evaluation and improvement of existing testing and measurement stations.
- Improve skills in **hardware/software design** and **mechanical design**.

- Evaluate the performance of developed test equipment and present findings to the development team.

## Requirements

- **Requirement:** Enrollment at university.

### Experience and Knowledge
- First experience in electrical and mechanical design.
- Knowledge of **object-oriented programming**.
- Work experience in a lab environment, data analysis, and measurement techniques (desirable).

### Personality and Working Practice
- Ability to take initiative and organize tasks effectively.
- Strong skill in structuring work towards goals.
- Clear communication with team members.

### Enthusiasm
- Interest in **MEMS technology**, **IoT-based products**, measurement technology development, and automation.
- Desire to contribute to a demanding team.

### Languages
- Fluent in **English**.

## Additional Information
- **Start:** October 2026
- **Duration:** 4 - 6 months
- **Application Documents:** Please attach CV, transcript of records, enrollment certificate, examination regulations, and (if indicated) a valid work and residence permit.
- **Diversity:** Bosch welcomes all applications regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Contact
Steffen Knecht (Functional Department)
+49 7121 35 6271
summary: Bosch is offering a 4-6 month internship starting in October 2026 focused on developing and automating test equipment for MEMS sensors and IoT devices. The role involves hardware/software design, data analysis, and mechanical design for students in Electrical Engineering, Physics, or Mechanical Engineering.
title: Internship in the Development of MEMS Test Equipment

description:
## Role Overview
At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference.

In many industrial and research applications, tracking millions of particles within a fluid flow is essential, but current CPU-based methods are too slow. This project offers an exciting opportunity to break that bottleneck by building a state-of-the-art solver that leverages the massive power of modern GPUs to accelerate simulations by orders of magnitude. Join us in developing a cutting-edge tool that integrates directly with the industry-standard Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) software OpenFOAM, and make a significant impact on what's possible in computational science.

## What You Will Do
As a part of your Master thesis, you will:
- Conduct a **literature review** on state-of-the-art methods for Lagrangian Particle Tracking, GPU computing in CFD and particle-wall interaction.
- Develop a **high-performance particle tracking solver** using Python and the NVIDIA Warp framework to run on the GPU.
- Implement a method for **collision detection** with complex 3D geometries and create a **pre-processing pipeline** to prepare simulation data (e.g., flow fields and meshes) from OpenFOAM for the GPU.
- Integrate the new solver into a standard **OpenFOAM workflow**, establishing a seamless data exchange.
- Validate and **benchmark** your solver's performance against OpenFOAM's native tools, quantifying the massive speedup.

## Requirements

### Education
- Master studies in the field of **Engineering, Physics or comparable**.

### Experience and Knowledge
- Profound knowledge of and experience in **numerical simulation methods**.
- Solid understanding of **continuum mechanics**.
- Very good programming skills in **Python**.
- Initial experience with **OpenFOAM, NVIDIA Warp and ParaView** is desirable.
- Ideally, initial experience with **Git** and working on **HPC clusters**.

### Personality and Working Practice
- Able to proactively develop new ideas, find solutions independently and complete tasks efficiently.
- Demonstrating a high degree of self-motivation.

### Languages
- **English**: Business fluent.
- **German**: Good.

## Additional Information
- **Work Routine**: Partially mobile working possible, ideally with 3 - 4 office days per week.
- **Start**: According to prior agreement.
- **Duration**: 6 months.
- **Mandatory Requirement**: Enrollment at university. Please attach CV, transcript of records, examination regulations and (if indicated) a valid work and residence permit.

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are not just trends for us but are firmly anchored in our corporate culture. Therefore, we welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin or sexual identity.

## Contact
Alexander Fuchs (Functional Department)
+49 711 811 93399
summary: Bosch is seeking a Master's student for a 6-month thesis project to develop a GPU-accelerated particle tracking solver for OpenFOAM. The role involves using Python and NVIDIA Warp to improve simulation speed and implementing collision detection for complex 3D geometries.
title: Master Thesis GPU-Accelerated Particle Simulation for OpenFOAM

## Company Overview

## Role Overview

## Company Overview

Anna Lipatov (Human Resources Department)

description:
## Company Overview
**Bei Bosch** gestalten wir Zukunft mit hochwertigen Technologien und Dienstleistungen, die Begeisterung wecken und das Leben der Menschen verbessern. Unser Versprechen an unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter steht dabei felsenfest: Wir wachsen gemeinsam, haben Freude an unserer Arbeit und inspirieren uns gegenseitig. Willkommen bei Bosch.

Die **Bosch Industriekessel GmbH** freut sich auf Ihre Bewerbung!

## Role Overview
Als **Software Architekt:in** sind Sie die treibende Kraft bei der Digitalisierung unserer Fertigung. Sie verantworten den Entwurf und die Implementierung von innovativen Softwarelösungen, die als Brücke zwischen der Produktion und dem digitalen Zwilling fungieren. Sie begleiten den gesamten Entwicklungszyklus von der Anforderungsanalyse bis zur Inbetriebnahme und gestalten aktiv die Industrie 4.0-Landschaft bei Bosch.

## Responsibilities
- Gestaltung der zentralen technischen Schnittstelle zwischen physischer Fertigung und digitalem Zwilling durch Entwurf von Datenmodellen und Architekturen für die nahtlose Integration und Visualisierung von Produktionsdaten.
- Durchführung detaillierter Prozessanalysen direkt im Fertigungsumfeld, Identifikation von Effizienzpotenzialen und Übersetzung dieser in präzise technische Anforderungen.
- Entwurf und Realisierung zukunftsfähiger Softwarearchitekturen, insbesondere für **Python-basierte Backend-Lösungen** zur Datenverarbeitung und moderne **Angular-Webapplikationen**.
- Sicherstellung einer intuitiven Bedienbarkeit und hohen Akzeptanz der Applikationen durch starken Fokus auf benutzeroptimierte Oberflächen (**UI/UX**) in Zusammenarbeit mit Anwendern aus der Fertigung.
- Konzeption und Verwaltung der erforderlichen Datenbankstrukturen zur Abbildung der Fertigungsprozesse und zur Speisung des digitalen Zwillings.
- Sicherstellung der Qualität, Stabilität und Skalierbarkeit der Anwendungen sowie Begleitung des Rollouts in den Werken.

## Requirements

### Education
- Abgeschlossenes Studium der **Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, des Maschinenbaus** oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit starkem Bezug zu Software.

### Experience and Know-how
- **Langjährige Berufserfahrung** in der Softwareentwicklung, idealerweise als Softwarearchitekt:in.
- Fundierte Kenntnisse in der **Full-Stack-Entwicklung**, insbesondere im Backend (**Python**) und Frontend (**Angular**).
- Expertise im Bereich Datenbanken (z.B. **PostgreSQL, MySQL**).
- Nachweisbare Erfahrung in der Analyse von Prozessen, vorzugsweise im Fertigungsumfeld.

### Nice-to-haves
- Kenntnisse über **Industrie 4.0, IoT-Plattformen** und Konzepte des **digitalen Zwillings**.
- **SAP-Kenntnisse** im Fertigungsumfeld (z.B. Module wie **SAP ME/MII** oder **PP**).

### Personality and Working Style
- Fähigkeit, komplexe Zusammenhänge schnell zu durchdringen.
- Souveräne Vermittlung zwischen IT-Anforderungen und Produktionsbedürfnissen.
- Eigenverantwortliche und zielorientierte Arbeitsweise.

### Languages
- Sehr gutes **Deutsch** und **Englisch** in Wort und Schrift.

## What We Offer
- Ein hochgradig innovatives Arbeitsumfeld zur Mitgestaltung der Fabrik der Zukunft.
- Ein engagiertes, interdisziplinäres Team.
- **Flexible Arbeitszeiten**.
- Möglichkeit zum **mobilen Arbeiten**.
- Eine **attraktive Vergütung**.
- Zahlreiche **Weiterbildungsangebote**.
summary: Bosch is seeking a Software Architect to lead the digitalization of its manufacturing processes by creating a bridge between physical production and digital twins. The role involves full-stack development using Python and Angular, process analysis in the factory environment, and managing database structures to enable Industry 4.0 capabilities.
title: Software Architekt (w/m/div.)

description:
## Role Overview
At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Robert Bosch GmbH is seeking an excellent PhD candidate to bridge the gap between modular pipelines and end-to-end learning in automated driving. Your goal is to be the architect of a next-generation, modular, and scalable end-to-end driving model (SEED), exploring the balance between data-driven power and safety-critical explainability. This position offers a bridge between academic excellence and industrial application, allowing you to publish research at top-tier venues while seeing contributions integrated into real-world ADAS products.

## Your Contribution to the Future of Driving
- **Master the State-of-the-Art:** Conduct a deep-dive analysis of current breakthroughs in end-to-end autonomous driving and investigate how to exploit large-scale datasets without relying on labeled data for every sub-task.
- **Innovate and Develop:** Design and implement novel deep neural network architectures that prioritize both high-performance scaling and human-interpretable explainability.
- **Experiment and Validate:** Validate the performance and reliability of your models through rigorous experiments on public benchmarks and massive, Bosch-owned autonomous driving datasets.
- **From Lab to Road:** Collaborate with expert project teams in deep learning and computer vision to brainstorm new ideas and deploy software on hardware, validating research on real automotive driving systems.
- **Share Your Success:** Publish findings and research outcomes at top-tier computer vision and machine learning journals and conferences.

## Requirements
- **Education:** Master degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science or similar with excellent grades.
- **Experience and Knowledge:** Detailed knowledge of **computer vision**, **3D vision**, **machine learning**, **deep learning**, **artificial intelligence** (Transformer, Sparse and BEV Queries) and experience with dedicated development tools (**TensorFlow**, **PyTorch**, **Python**).
- **Personality and Working Practice:** High level of enthusiasm, creativity, ability to work independently with excellent task-management, team spirit, assertiveness, and persuasiveness.
- **Enthusiasm:** Interest in conceptual work and method-based design.
- **Languages:** Fluent in English and German written and spoken.

## Additional Information
- **Final Topic:** Depends on your university.
- **Start:** By prior agreement.
- **Application:** Please submit CV, certificates, and links to GitHub or Kaggle accounts.
- **Diversity:** Bosch welcomes all applications regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.
summary: Bosch is looking for a PhD candidate to develop a scalable and explainable end-to-end driving model (SEED). The role involves researching deep neural network architectures for autonomous driving, validating them on real-world datasets, and publishing findings in top-tier academic venues.
title: PhD Thesis - Scalable and explainable End2End Driving Model (SEED)

## Company Overview
**Bosch Sensortec GmbH** is a technology leader in sensing solutions based on microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) and dedicated to the consumer electronics world. They develop and market key technologies for smartphones, tablets, hearables, wearables, smartglasses, augmented and virtual reality applications, gaming devices, and more. Bosch Sensortec GmbH is a wholly owned subsidiary of Robert Bosch GmbH.

## Role Overview
Bosch is looking for a highly motivated and creative individual (m/f/d) for an engineering position focused on the **integration and characterization of acoustic transducers** in complex MEMS systems. This role contributes to innovative projects in consumer electronics and IoT, developing new applications for intelligent MEMS sensors and actuators.

## What You Will Do / Responsibilities

- Generate insightful, data-driven conclusions through the **modeling, characterization, and validation** of MEMS devices.

- Integrate acoustic transducers, define test concepts, and connect them to sensor system modeling.

### Experience & Knowledge
- **Experience:** Several years of work experience in the field of acoustics. Experience in sensor characterization, system integration, or measurement systems is a strong plus.

description:
## Company Overview
**At Bosch**, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference.

## Role Overview
In this internship you will develop an AI-assisted pipeline that converts technical documents into structured engineering knowledge. The project focuses on applying machine learning and document understanding techniques to automatically identify, extract, validate, and structure material parameters from supplier documentation. The goal is to build a reproducible data pipeline that transforms unstructured documents into a structured knowledge base used in engineering simulations.

## What You Will Do
- Develop a **document understanding pipeline** to identify relevant parameter tables in technical documents.
- Implement **machine learning / LLM-based extraction** of material parameters and metadata.
- Design a **structured representation** of material knowledge including provenance and validation rules.
- Build **automated workflows** that convert extracted data into simulation-ready datasets.
- Implement **quality checks and anomaly detection** for extracted parameters.
- Explore methods to improve extraction accuracy using ML techniques (**prompting, classification, model evaluation**).
- Create tools that allow engineers to review and approve extracted information efficiently.
- Document the workflow and evaluate the performance of the developed system.

## Requirements
- **Education**: Studies in the field of Electrical Engineering, Physics, Computer Science, Data Science or comparable.
- **Experience and Knowledge**: Experience with automation, data processing, LLMs, Python, Git, Linux.
- **Personality and Working Practice**: Approach tasks in a structured manner and develop solutions independently.
- **Work Routine**: On-site presence is required.

description:
## Role Overview
**Process & Maintenance Engineer Automation / Transportsystem (w/m/div.)**

Join Robert Bosch Semiconductor Manufacturing Dresden GmbH and help transform ideas into useful and meaningful technologies across Mobility Solutions, Consumer Goods, Industrial Technology, or Energy and Building Technology to improve quality of life worldwide.

## Responsibilities
- Ensure **production availability** and the professional, qualitative execution of maintenance measures for automation systems (including **Sorter, FOUPCleaner, Stocker, Reticle-Stocker, Marker**) within a full continuous shift system.
- Assess wafer manufacturing status during system errors, recover wafers with appropriate reprocessing, and provide documentation.
- Monitor **Wafer in Process (WIP)** and ensure timely processing of hold material.
- Independently perform qualifications based on specified instructions using **Statistical Process Control (SPC)** and **Fault Detection and Classification (FDC)** methods.
- Ensure correct setup states in production, continuously monitor hold material, and process it according to **OCAP**.
- Perform planned and unplanned maintenance while adhering to safety regulations and work instructions.
- Conduct troubleshooting and rectification of **electrical, electronic, and mechanical systems** using reaction plans, collaborating with internal teams and external service partners.
- Support the creation of handling instructions and associated checklists for maintenance measures.

## Requirements

### Education
- Successfully completed technical vocational training as an **Electrician, Mechatronics Technician, or Mechanic**.
- Ideally supplemented by professional experience in the semiconductor industry or a technician's degree / Master Craftsman (Meister) qualification.

### Experience and Know-how
- Professional experience in a comparable position.
- Desirable: Specific knowledge of semiconductor processes in a production area or experience with equipment from at least one manufacturer.

### Personality & Working Style
- **Personality:** Team player, flexible, motivated, resilient, and responsible.
- **Working Style:** Independent, self-reliant, committed, solution-oriented, and reliable.
- Comfortable working at heights within the transport system (up to 5m).

### Languages
- **German:** Very good knowledge (sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse).
- **English:** Good written and spoken knowledge.

## Contact Information

- **Technical Questions:** Ralph Wichtendahl (Technical Department) +49 351 8547-3610

description:
## Company Overview
**Bosch** designs the future with high-quality technologies and services that evoke enthusiasm and improve people's lives. The **Hörburger GmbH**, which has been part of Bosch Sicherheitssysteme GmbH since March 2022, offers comprehensive solutions in intelligent building and plant automation, control cabinet construction, retail facility management, energy management, and machine automation.

## Role Overview
Join a team of over 3,600 employees across more than 50 locations in Germany. As consultants, installers, and service providers, the team develops and implements networked solutions in building security, building automation, and energy efficiency based on the latest technologies.

## Requirements
- Sound knowledge in the development of **APIs**, specifically **REST** and/or **GraphQL**.
- Proven experience in developing applications in **cloud environments** such as **Kubernetes**, **Microsoft Azure**, **GCP**, and/or **AWS**.
- Proficiency with **SOLID principles** and **Clean Code** practices.
- In-depth understanding and application of **Software Design Patterns**.
- Strong architectural understanding and the ability to conceptualize complex software solutions.
- Sound knowledge of at least one modern **Java framework** such as **Spring** (especially **Spring Boot**), **Quarkus**, and/or **Micronaut**.
- Experience with modularization concepts such as **Microservices**, **Modulith**, or **Function-as-a-Service (FaaS)**.
- Experienced use of test automation tools such as **JUnit** and **Mockito**.
- Familiarity with modern, **Azure-based services**, event-driven software designs, and toolkits for the entire software development lifecycle.

## Nice-to-Haves / Additional Qualifications
- Experience with **NoSQL databases**.
- Practical experience in **CI/CD** (e.g., **Jenkins**, **GitLab CI**, **Azure DevOps**).
- Knowledge of **containerization** (e.g., **Docker**, **Podman**).
- Experience with **time-series databases** and their processing.
- Familiarity with monitoring tools like **Grafana**.
- Initial experience with **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)** tools such as **Pulumi**.
- Interest and/or experience in the field of **IoT**.

## Company Culture
Diversity and inclusion are firmly anchored in the corporate culture. Applications are welcomed regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin, or sexual identity.

## Contact Person
Julia Hagenlocher (Human Resources Department)
+49 (0) 83152241-70

description:
We’re looking for a **Data Platform Engineer (m/f/d)** to join our team and help build and operate the data infrastructure that powers Flix’s mobility platform.

At FlixTech, our data platform enables teams across the company to process, analyze, and act on large volumes of mobility data. The platform supports critical use cases such as network planning, pricing, operations, and business analytics. You will work on scalable infrastructure that processes millions of daily events and ensures reliable data availability across the organization.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional engineering environment collaborating with data analysts, data scientists, and platform engineers. The role focuses on building reliable, scalable data systems and enabling other teams to work efficiently with data.

## About the Role
- Design, build, and maintain scalable data infrastructure running on **AWS** and **Kubernetes**
- Develop and operate streaming and data platform components using technologies such as **Kafka** and **Snowflake**
- Implement monitoring, alerting, and operational tooling to ensure platform reliability and performance
- Own features end-to-end, including development, testing, deployment, monitoring, and maintenance
- Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code using **Terraform** and **GitLab CI/CD**
- Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and stakeholders to support data platform use cases
- Contribute to improving platform reliability, automation, and engineering practices
- Participate in on-call rotations to support the stability of the data platform

## About You
- **3+ years of experience** building or operating data infrastructure or data platforms
- Hands-on experience with streaming technologies such as **Kafka** and data warehouses like **Snowflake**
- Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools (e.g., **Terraform**) and CI/CD pipelines (e.g., **GitLab**)
- Practical experience with cloud platforms (preferably **AWS**) and container platforms such as **Kubernetes**
- Experience with monitoring and observability tools (e.g., **Datadog** or similar)
- Solid programming skills in **Python** and interest in writing clean, maintainable code
- Strong collaboration and communication skills when working with technical and business stakeholders
- Fluent in **English**

### Nice to have:
- Experience with advanced **SQL** or data transformation tools (e.g., **dbt**)
- Experience with data processing frameworks (e.g., **PySpark**)
- Knowledge of security and networking concepts relevant for data platforms

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

## Why Join Flix?
At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

description:
At Flix, we offer a tech-driven environment where innovation meets real-world impact, with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a culture of collaboration and ownership.

As a **Full-stack Engineer** with a strong backend focus (**Kotlin**) you will help us build the future of payments for green and smart mobility.

You’ll join our **Payments Team** - the heart of our checkout experience - as part of our Demand Division. Your work will directly impact how millions of people pay, how quickly their transactions are processed, and how much revenue we generate. We continuously work to improve payment authorization and conversion rates, increase payment efficiency and revenue protection, and ensure data reliability. At the same time, we’re integrating local payment methods across different markets and tackling complex engineering challenges along the way.

We build our core services using **Kotlin and Spring Boot**, and our interfaces with **TypeScript and React.js**. We also maintain some legacy **PHP** systems — you don’t need to love it, but you should be open to working with it when needed.

Our Payments Team consists of four backend engineers, two frontend engineers and an Engineering Manager.

## About the Role
- Build the systems that move money fast, safely, and at scale
- Design and ship features used by millions of users
- Write clean, tested, production-ready code
- Own features from idea to release
- Work closely with business, UX, frontend, and backend engineers
- Review code, pair program, and ship often
- Improve existing systems and clean up legacy code

## About You
- **3-4 years of experience** building backend or full-stack web applications, including at least 2 years working with Kotlin in a commercial setup
- Some experience with frontend development with **JavaScript/Typescript** and any modern framework (**React.js, Vue.js, Angular**)
- Open to working with **PHP** in legacy systems when needed - prior experience is not required, the team will support your learning
- Strong object-oriented programming skills with a focus on clean architecture and maintainable design
- Solid experience working with relational databases
- Hands-on experience with automated testing (unit, integration, component or UI)
- Hands-on experience deploying applications to production environments using CI/CD pipelines
- Clear written and spoken English communication skills
- A collaborative, team-first mindset

## Nice to have
(We’ll support you in developing these skills if needed):
- Containerization and orchestration tools such as **Docker and k8s**
- Familiarity with event-driven architectures and messaging systems (e.g., **Kafka**)
- Exposure to **AWS** or other cloud platforms

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Fl

description:
We’re looking for a talented mid-level **Kotlin Engineer (m/f/d)** to join us in Berlin or Munich.

In the Foundation Division of FlixTech, our Finance Technology Stream helps the business grow efficiently by delivering smart, compliant, and automated finance solutions. We keep things simple for our internal users so they can spend less time on manual tasks and more time on what matters. The team you will join is not focused on user-facing experiences or polished UIs. Our success is measured by data correctness, consistency, and trustworthiness, even in complex and evolving business scenarios. You’ll be part of a self-managed, cross-functional team with a Product Owner and fellow Software Engineers, directly supporting new country launches, new business models, and the continuous evolution of Flix’s financial backbone.

This role is well-suited for someone who enjoys ownership, working close to real business problems, and contributing to the long-term foundations of an international company. If you value collaboration, thoughtful engineering, and building systems that need to be right, not just fast, we’d love to meet you.

## About the Role
- Design, develop, and maintain **backend services** using **Kotlin**
- Build and evolve **scalable, event-driven systems** that process, validate, and expose financial data
- Own features end-to-end: from **technical design and implementation** to **testing, deployment, monitoring, and ongoing maintenance**
- Collaborate closely with the Product Owner and Finance stakeholders to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions
- Ensure **data accuracy, traceability, and auditability** across financial and accounting flows
- Improve system reliability, observability, and performance for business-critical processes
- Contribute to **clean code, architectural discussions, and continuous improvement** of engineering practices

## About You
- **3 years of experience** as a Software Engineer working with **Kotlin**
- Hands-on experience building backend services using the **Spring ecosystem** (e.g. **Spring Boot, Spring Data**)
- Experience with **event-driven design** (e.g. asynchronous processing, messaging, domain events)
- Practical experience with **cloud platforms** (preferably **AWS**, or similar)
- Solid understanding of **databases**, both **NoSQL** (e.g. **DynamoDB**) and/or relational systems
- Comfortable working in **complex, data-heavy domains** (ideally Finance), where investigation, analysis, and precision are key
- Communicate clearly in **English**, open-minded, curious, and motivated to continuously improve both yourself and the systems you work on

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

## Why Join Flix?
At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

description:
## Role Overview
We are seeking a talented and driven **Senior Full Stack Engineer (m/f/d)** to join our team in the **Demand Division**. You will be responsible for developing our contact center applications, focusing on scalable agent-facing interfaces and automations. This is a unique opportunity to be part of a development team evolving software products on a scale, involving significant discoveries in the design process to identify the most efficient and effective workflows.

## Tech Stack
- **Languages & Frameworks**: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, flask, FastAPI, Node.js, React, Spring Boot
- **Cloud Providers and IaC**: AWS, Azure, terraform, serverless
- **Data Layer**: Elasticsearch, Snowflake
- **Streaming and Messaging Layer**: Kafka
- **Observability**: Datadog, Kibana

## About the Role
- You will be part of a self-organized, cross-functional distributed team-owning set of products and will hence directly contribute to the success of the business.
- You will maintain a core product built with **TypeScript** and **Java / Spring Boot**.
- You and your team will place our users at the center when developing solutions together.
- You drive the development process using an **Agile environment**.
- You will be able to evolve products using new tech stacks.
- Lead technical decisions inside and across teams to improve technical excellence through lateral leadership.

## About You
- A minimum of **8+ years of professional experience** in fullstack development, with a strong focus on architecture, system design, and migrating legacy products.
- You have strong experience in maintaining and evolving products written in **Python, Java / Spring Boot**, enabling their migration to a different tech stack.
- You cover your code with tests and have experience with different test runners.
- Your obsession with quality ensures every product meets exceptionally high standards.
- You enjoy working within an agile setup.

*We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply.*

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: Office-first company, but offering flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

## Why Join Flix?
At Flix, we empower our teams to push boundaries and shape the future of mobility. As we continue to scale globally, we harness cutting-edge technology to make mobility smarter, more sustainable, and more affordable. If you’re looking for a place where you can drive change and redefine how millions of people travel, Flix is the place where you can lead your journey!

description:
## Senior Full-Stack Engineer (m/f/d) - Station Management & Inventory

We are looking for a motivated and curious **Senior Full-Stack Engineer (Java/Kotlin)** to join our team in Supply Division and drive customer station experience to the next level!

In an ever growing network, Flix customers worldwide expect a comfortable and seamless experience at our stations as part of their travel experience. Our self-organized, cross-functional and distributed team builds products to enable Flix station managers to scale management of the station inventory, which a wide range of internal tech teams across Flix depend on, and overall serves to improve the experience at our stations for millions of passengers.

In this role, you will continue to shape the product by driving innovation, solving complex challenges, and creating a lasting impact on our organization.

## About the Role
- You take ownership of roadmap epics as topic driver, aligning technical requirements and solutions across the team together with the product owner
- As part of the team you will work closely with business stakeholders on continuously developing and improving the product vision
- You always keep the business value in mind when making decisions
- You drive the development process using an agile environment
- As part of your daily work, you collaborate closely with your fellow team members, for example in pair and ensemble programming, doing code reviews, testing, and operations
- You frequently deliver new versions of the product, using continuous integration and delivery
- You work confidently with or learn how to use cutting-edge technologies and tools including but not limited to:
    - **Kotlin** across server and browser
    - **JVM** on server-side (**Ktor**, **Axon Framework**)
    - Component-based Frontend (**Compose HTML**, or similar libraries and frameworks)
    - Distributed system architecture (**Apache Kafka**, **HTTP/REST**)
    - Infrastructure with **CI/CD** (**AWS**, **Docker**, **Kubernetes**, **Terraform**, **Datadog**, **Gitlab**)

## About You
- **5+ years of software engineering experience**, primarily backend development with a JVM language
- Willingness to work with **Kotlin**
- Solid front-end skills with modern front-end stack
- Experience with **event-driven architectures**; exposure to **event sourcing**, **CQRS**, and **domain-driven design (DDD)** is a plus
- Hands-on experience with automation and deployment of applications into cloud infrastructure (e.g., **AWS**, **Azure**) using **CI/CD pipelines** and **DevOps best practices**
- Proficiency in **system design and architecture**
- You take responsibility for the product and technical decisions
- Clear written and spoken **English** communication skills

*We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply.*

## What We Offer
- **Travel perks**: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
- **Work from (M)Anywhere**: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
- **Hybrid work model**: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
- **Wellbeing support**: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
- **Learning & Development**: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
- **Mentoring Program**: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.

## Why Join Flix?
At Flix, we empower our teams to push boundaries and shape the future of mobility. As we continue to scale globally, we harness cutting-edge technology to make mobility smarter, more sustainable, and more affordable.

If you’re looking for a place where you can drive change and redefine how millions of people travel, Flix is the place where you can lead your journey!

description:
## Warum Scout24?

**Über Sprengnetter eine Tochter der Scout24-Gruppe:**
Sprengnetter, seit 2023 Teil der Scout24-Gruppe, vereinfacht seit 1978 die Immobilienbewertung und macht sie sicherer sowie effizienter. Das Unternehmen unterstützt Partner in der Kredit- und Immobilienwirtschaft mit innovativen Softwarelösungen, aktuellen Marktdaten, professionellen Dienstleistungen, Fachliteratur sowie vielfältigen Weiterbildungsangeboten. Durch Tochterunternehmen in Österreich und Italien treibt Sprengnetter zudem die Internationalisierung seines Geschäfts voran. Mit über 200 Mitarbeitenden setzt das Unternehmen Maßstäbe in der Branche.

Wenn das für dich interessant klingt, freuen wir uns, dich bei uns zu begrüßen! Auch wenn du vielleicht nicht alle Anforderungen der Stelle erfüllst, sind wir gespannt auf den Mehrwert, den du in unser Team einbringen kannst. Wachse mit uns! Willkommen zu Hause!

Neben unseren Mitarbeiter:innen und unserer Unternehmenskultur bieten wir tolle Benefits, die Scout24 zu einem großartigen Arbeitsplatz machen!

- **Gehalt:** Ein wettbewerbsfähiges Gehaltspaket
- **Altersvorsorge:** Wir bieten eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge an und unterstützen deine eigene Beitragszahlung mit einem attraktiven Zuschuss.
- **Flexibilität:** Entscheide selbst, wo du am effektivsten arbeitest. Wir bieten dir ein hybrides Arbeitsmodell mit zwei Präsenztagen pro Woche im Büro und die restlichen Tage darfst du dir frei einteilen. Wir bieten dir 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr
- **Dein neues Büro:** Unser Büro auf dem Sprengnetter-Campus, in zentraler Lage im Herzen Bad Neuenahr-Ahrweilers mit wenigen Gehminuten zu lokalen Einkaufsmöglichkeiten und eigenem Bistro, bietet höhenverstellbare Schreibtische, mit einer Dachterrasse mit Blick auf die Weinberge Ahrweilers.
- **Gesundheit:** Urban Sports Club, regelmäßige Gesundheitschecks
- **Nutze unsere Produkte:** Als Mitarbeiter:in kannst du unsere Plus-Mitgliedschaft für Mieter:innen auf ImmoScout24 nutzen, um eine neue Wohnung zu finden.
- **Mobilität:** Wir bezuschussen Job Bikes und bieten kostenfreie Ladestationen für E-Bikes und -PKW.
- **Weiterbildung:** Umfassendes, zertifiziertes und international anerkanntes Weiterbildungsangebot der Sprengnetter Akademie

## Was wünschen wir uns von Dir?

- Sicherstellung des Betriebs unserer IT-Services mit Anforderungen zur **Hochverfügbarkeit, Skalierbarkeit, Stabilität und Sicherheit**
- Weiterentwicklung unserer **Monitoringkonzepte** und Umsetzung dieser in die Praxis
- Verantwortung für den Aufbau und die Weiterentwicklung der **Build- und Deployment-Pipelines**
- Überführung von **On-Premise-Systemen in eine ganzheitliche Cloud-Umgebung**
- Erstellung, systematische und nachvollziehbare Pflege von **Betriebsdokumentationen**
- Kommunikation und Zusammenarbeit mit unseren Kunden und internen Stakeholdern
- Mitarbeit in technischen Teilprojekten

## Was bringst Du schon mit?

- Weitreichende Erfahrung in **DevOps-Themen** und Bereitschaft, diese in einem übergreifenden Team einzubringen
- Fundierte Kenntnisse im Betrieb von **hochverfügbaren Systemarchitekturen** sowie **Datenbanksystemen** (z. B. PostgresSql, MariaDB)
- **Technologie-Stack:** PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Git, Jenkins, Ansible, Docker
- Analytische Denkweise, lösungsorientiertes und strukturiertes Vorgehen
- Motivation und eigenverantwortliche, proaktive Arbeitsweise, klare Kundenorientierung
- **gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse** in Wort und Schrift

Wir möchten die Vielfalt unseres Unternehmens weiter ausbauen und Menschen mit Behinderung entsprechend beruflich fördern. Wir haben eine Schwerbehindertenvertretung, die Dich im weiteren Bewerbungsprozess unterstützt. Solltest Du diese Unterstützung benötigen, gib uns bitte im Voraus Bescheid.

The role focuses on building deployment pipelines, implementing monitoring concepts, and managing a tech stack including Docker, Jenkins, and Ansible.
